title: Siglec-10 antibodies
abstract: This disclosure relates to agents that bind and neutralize the inhibitory activity of Siglec-10 in lymphocytes, notably by inhibiting the binding of Siglec-10 to its sialic acid ligands on target cells, notably tumor cells. Such agents can be used for the treatment of cancers.
claims:
Claims 1. A monoclonal antibody or antibody fragment that specifically binds to a human Siglec-10 polypeptide, wherein the antibody is capable of inhibiting the interactions between a Siglec-10 polypeptide and a human cancer cell. 2. A monoclonal antibody or antibody fragment that specifically binds to a human Siglec-10 polypeptide expressed at the surface of a cell and neutralizes the inhibitory activity of the Siglec-10 polypeptide. 3. The antibody or antibody fragment of claims 1 or 2, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is capable of inhibiting the interactions between a Siglec-10 polypeptide and a human MDA-MB-231 cell. 4. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is capable of inhibiting the interactions between a Siglec- 10 polypeptide and a human A375 cell. 5. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the blocking and/or neutralizing activity of the antibody or antibody fragment does not depend on the ability of the antibody or antibody fragment to induce or increase down-modulation of Siglec-10 at the surface of a cell. 6. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment lacks an Fc domain or comprises a human Fc domain that is modified to reduce binding between the Fc domain and an Fey receptor. 7. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is an lgG1 and comprises one, two or three substitutions at residues 234, 235, 237, 297, 322, 330 (Kabat EU numbering). 8. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment comprises two antigen binding domains that each bind to a Siglec-10 polypeptide. 9. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein 

 the antibody or antibody fragment does not induce inhibitory signalling by Siglec-10 polypeptide expressed by a cell. 10. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment does not substantially bind to any of the human Siglec-5, - 7 or -1 1 polypeptides. 1 1 . The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above, which inhibits activation of the Siglec-10 by a sialic acid ligand of the Siglec-10 present on a cell, optionally wherein the ligand is present on the surface of a tumor cell. 12. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment causes an increase in a marker associated with activation and/or cytotoxicity in a Siglec-10 expressing lymphocyte, when the lymphocyte is brought into contact with a target human cell bearing a ligand of the Siglec-10 on the target cell surface. 13. An isolated monoclonal antibody or antibody fragment characterized by: a) specifically binding to Siglec-10 polypeptide, and when bound to Siglec-10 on a human leukocyte, increasing activation and/or cytotoxicity of the leukocyte toward a target human cell bearing a sialic acid ligand of Siglec-10 on the target cell surface, when said target cell comes into contact with the leukocyte; b) not substantially binding to human CD16A, CD16B, CD32A, CD32B and/or CD64; and c) optionally, not substantially causing the down-modulation of Siglec-10 expression on the surface of a cell and/or not being reliant on the ability to cause the down-modulation of Siglec-10 expression on the surface of a cell for the ability to increase activation and/or cytotoxicity of the leukocyte. 14. The antibody or antibody fragment of claims 1 1-13, wherein the tumor cell or target cell is a human MDA-MB-231 cell or a human A375 cell. 15. The antibody or antibody fragment of claims 13 or 14, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is capable of blocking the interactions between a Siglec-10 polypeptide and a human MDA-MB-231 cell or a human A375 cell. 16. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, that 

 competes for binding to Siglec-10 with an antibody selected from the group consisting of: (a) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 12 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 13; (b) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 20 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 21 ; and (c) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 28 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 29. 17. An antibody or antibody fragment selected from the group consisting of: (a) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 12 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 13; (b) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 20 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 21 ; and (c) a monoclonal antibody comprising (i) a heavy chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the heavy chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 28 and (ii) a light chain comprising CDR 1 , 2 and 3 of the light chain variable region of SEQ ID NO: 29. 18. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein said antibody is a chimeric, human or humanized antibody or antibody fragment. 19. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is a multispecific, optionally bispecific, antibody that comprises a first antigen binding domain that binds Siglec-10 and a second antigen binding domain that binds a protein of interest other than Siglec-10. 20. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is a non-depleting antibody. 21 . The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 1-20, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is a human lgG1 isotype antibody or an antibody having an Fc 

 domain that is modified to reduce binding between the Fc domain and an Fey receptor. 22. The antibody fragment of claims 1-20, wherein said antibody fragment is an antibody fragment selected from Fab, Fab', Fab'-SH, F(ab') 2, Fv, a diabody, a single- chain antibody fragment, or a multispecific antibody comprising multiple different antibody fragments. 23. The antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, wherein said antibody or antibody fragment is conjugated or covalently bound to a detectable moiety. 24. An antibody or antibody fragment obtained by chimerizing or humanizing an antibody or antibody fragment of any of one claims 1 to 22. 25. A pharmaceutical composition comprising an antibody or antibody fragment according to any one of the above claims, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 26. A kit comprising the antibody or antibody fragment of any one of the above claims, optionally further comprising a labeled secondary antibody that specifically recognizes the antibody of any one of the above claims. 27. A nucleic acid encoding a heavy and/or light chain of an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 to 24. 28. A hybridoma or recombinant host cell producing the antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 to 24. 29. A method for the treatment or prevention of a disease in a patient in need thereof, the method comprising administering to said patient an effective amount of an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1-24 or a composition of claim 25. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein said disease is cancer, optionally a carcinoma. 31 . The method of claim 30, wherein the cancer or carcinoma is selected from a colorectal cancer, breast cancer or malignant melanoma. 32. A method for the treatment or prevention of a cancer in a patient in need thereof, the method comprising administering to said patient an effective amount of an antibody or antibody fragment that binds human Siglec-10 at the surface of a cell and that is capable of neutralizing the inhibitory activity thereof, wherein the an antibody comprises an Fc domain that is modified to reduce binding between the Fc domain and an Fey receptor. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the antibody or antibody fragment is an lgG1 and comprises one, two or three substitutions at residues 234, 235 and 331 or at 234, 235 and 322 (EU numbering). 34. A method for modulating the activity of lymphocytes in a subject the method comprising administering to said subject an effective amount of an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 -24 or a composition of claim 25. 35. An in vitro method for modulating the activity of leukocytes, the method comprising bringing leukocytes expressing Siglec-10 into contact with an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1-24 or a composition of claim 25. 36. An in vitro method for assessing the activity of leukocytes from a subject having disease, the method comprising obtaining a biological sample from a subject comprising leukocytes, bringing the leukocytes into contact with an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1-24 and assessing whether the antibody or antibody fragment modulates the activity of the leukocytes. 37. The method of claims 35-36, wherein the leukocytes are present in a biological sample obtained from a subject having a disease, optionally a cancer. 38. A method for selecting subjects having a cancer that responds to a treatment with an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 -24, the method comprising determining whether cancer cells in said subject express a ligand of Siglec-10, optionally whether cancer cells express elevated levels of a ligand Siglec-10, the expression of sialic acid ligand(s) of Siglec-10, or elevated levels of sialic acid ligand(s) of Siglec-10, being indicative of a responder subject. 39. The method of claim 38, further comprising administering to a responder subject an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1 -24 or a composition of claim 25. 40. A method for treating a cancer in an individual, the method comprising administering to said individual an effective amount of an antibody or antibody fragment of any one of claims 1-24 or a composition of claim 25, in combination with an antibody that neutralizes the inhibitory activity of human PD-1. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the cancer is a carcinoma. 42. A method of producing an antibody or antibody fragment which binds Siglec-10 and which neutralizes the inhibitory activity of Siglec-10, said method comprising the steps of: (a) providing a plurality of antibodies or antibody fragments that bind a Siglec- 10 polypeptide, (b) selecting an antibody or antibody fragment that binds the Siglec-10 polypeptide, (c) selecting an antibody or antibody fragment that neutralizes the inhibitory activity of the Siglec-10 polypeptide, and (d) optionally, selecting an antibody or antibody fragment that inhibits the binding of the Siglec-10 polypeptide to a human tumor cell bearing sialic acid ligands of Siglec- 10. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein step (c) comprises selecting an antibody or antibody fragment capable of causing an increase in a marker associated with cytotoxicity in a Siglec-10-expressing lymphocyte, when the lymphocyte is brought into contact with a target human cell bearing a ligand of the Siglec-10 on the target cell surface.
